rulings.Case Title: JOHN A KITHAS VS. LARRY WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate “Order For Sale Of Dwelling” Filed September 27, 2022
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANTS LARRY WONG AND ANNA WONG'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE AND VACATE "ORDER FOR SALE OF DWELLING" FILED SEPTEMBER 27, 2022 is continued to December 15, 2022 for the moving party to provide courtesy copies of their filings in support of this motion (opposition received). The Court notes that no courtesy copies by the moving party are received for November 23, 2022 motion either. All courtesy copies for both motions shall be delivered to Department 501 no later than November 28, 2022 with cover letters reflecting the new hearing date. The new hearing date for both motions is December 15, 2022. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY** ONCORE LIVING, LLC ET AL VS. BARBARY COAST LIVING
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Defendants Motion To Quash Service And/Or Strike Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction [C.C.P. 418.10]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2022 line 6. NOTICE OF MOTION FOR DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE AND/OR STRIKE COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION is DENIED. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that one of the Plaintiff's status as a suspended corporation deprives the Court of jurisdiction over the defendants. Any arguments regarding Plaintiff's status, Plaintiff's standing, statute of limitations, etc. must be brought via an appropriate motion under the Code of Civil Procedure. Motion to Quash is not such motion. The Court notes that Plaintiff's complaint fails to allege any facts that San Francisco is the proper venue for this case. In fact, Complaint expressly alleges that the proper venue is County of Los Angeles. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NICOLA VOLPI ET AL VS. DESIGN LINE CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend to allege facts, not conclusions, as to each element of the misrepresentation/concealment cause of action as to each misrepresentation and concealment (e.g. "brand new ground up home" - statement was false because a pre-existing house on the lot was remodeled - defendant knew that an old house on the lot was remodeled - Plaintiff believed this representation - Plaintiff sustained specified damages because the house was not "brand new ground up home). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2022 line 12. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR ALTERNATIVELY SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, IN FAVOR OF DEFENDANTS is OFF CALENDAR. No valid proof of service on file. No method of service indicated in the declared part of the document. rulings.Case Title: 16-50 LAGUNA I2, LP VS. GEORGE M. GRAGO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for November 21, 2022 line 14. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile allegations in the complaint asserting service by posting on September 13, 2022 and POS for service on September 20, 2022. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, the requirement to comply with the fictitious business names is a question of fact, not properly addressed on this demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
